title: Henckelia crenata
taxonomy: Henckelia crenata is classified within the family Gesneriaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This plant is part of the genus Henckelia, which is native to Southeast Asia. The genus Henckelia is closely related to the genus Chirita, with some sources considering them to be the same. The exact classification of Henckelia crenata may vary depending on the source, but it is generally classified as a species within the Gesneriaceae family. The classification of plants can sometimes be subject to change as new research emerges.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
